part.p: If a mapping is not clear, the provider should supply new information indicating that the Key Security Indicator has not been independently audited.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers seeking a FedRAMP Rev5 Class A Certification by leveraging an alternative security framework that is based on the SP 800-53 Revision 5 MUST supply all Security Decision Record materials required for FedRAMP Rev5 Class B Certification.

part.name: guidance
part.p: The only approved alternative security frameworks based on the SP 800-53 Revision 5 are FedRAMP Ready and GovRAMP.

part.name: guidance
part.p: An independent assessment is not required for FedRAMP Rev5 Class A Certification.

part.name: overview
part.p: These rules apply to cloud service providers who have met all other relevant rules and are ready to apply for any FedRAMP Certification.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers MUST be listed in the FedRAMP Marketplace before applying for FedRAMP Certification.

part.name: guidance
part.p: See FedRAMP's Marketplace Listing rules for information about being listed in the Marketplace in the Preparation Phase prior to receiving a formal FedRAMP Certification.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers MUST complete the FedRAMP Certification Application Form at https://fedramp.gov/forms/provider-listing-request/ in full to request an initial assessment by FedRAMP.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers MUST supply a fresh initial FedRAMP Certification Package that shows the current status of the cloud service offering as verified and validated by the provider within the previous 7 days.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers MUST supply a fresh initial independent verification and validation assessment that was completed by a FedRAMP Recognized Independent Assessment Service within the previous 3 months.

group.title: Applying for FedRAMP Certification with an Agency Sponsor

part.name: overview
part.p: These rules apply to cloud service providers with an Agency Sponsor who have met all other relevant rules and are ready to apply for any FedRAMP Certification.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers seeking a FedRAMP Rev5 Agency Certification MUST have completed the Authorization to Operate (ATO) process with their agency sponsor for the cloud service offering, concluding with a formal signed ATO letter that the agency has sent over official government channels to FedRAMP.

part.name: overview
part.p: These rules apply to providers for FedRAMP 20x Certifications.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers MUST maintain simple high-level summaries of at least the following for each Key Security Indicator:

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers SHOULD apply ALL Key Security Indicators to ALL aspects of their cloud service offering that are within the FedRAMP Minimum Assessment Scope.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers of FedRAMP 20x Class A offerings SHOULD verify and validate the status of machine-based information resources at least once every month.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers of FedRAMP 20x Class B offerings MUST verify and validate the status of machine-based information resources at least once every 7 days.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers of FedRAMP 20x Class C offerings MUST verify and validate the status of machine-based information resources at least once every 3 days.

part.name: overview
part.p: These rules apply to independent assessment services supporting FedRAMP 20x Certifications.

part.name: statement
part.p: Assessors MUST NOT rely on screenshots, configuration dumps, or other static output as evidence EXCEPT when evaluating the accuracy and reliability of a process that generates such artifacts.

part.name: statement
part.p: Assessors MUST verify and validate the underlying processes (both machine-based and non-machine-based) that providers use to validate Key Security Indicators; this should include at least:

part.name: overview
part.p: These rules apply to providers for FedRAMP Rev5 Certifications.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers seeking a FedRAMP Rev5 Class D Certification MUST use the FedRAMP Agency Certification path.

part.name: guidance
part.p: FedRAMP will not perform FedRAMP Rev5 Class D Program Certifications.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers of FedRAMP Rev5 Class A offerings SHOULD verify and validate the status of machine-based information resources at least once every month.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers of FedRAMP Rev5 Class B offerings SHOULD verify and validate the status of machine-based information resources at least once every month.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers of FedRAMP Rev5 Class C offerings MUST verify and validate the status of machine-based information resources at least once every month.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers of FedRAMP Rev5 Class D offerings MUST verify and validate the status of machine-based information resources at least once every month.

part.name: statement
part.p: Providers with FedRAMP Rev5 Ready status MUST convert to a FedRAMP Certification before the furthest date of the expiration of their most recently yearly assessment or November 17, 2026; the legacy FedRAMP Ready status will be entirely removed on December 31, 2027.

part.name: guidance
part.p: Cloud services that do not wish to convert or do not meet conversion criteria will be renamed Legacy FedRAMP Ready and otherwise retired from FedRAMP Ready.

part.name: overview
part.p: The Security Decision Record replaced a traditional System Security Plan with a persistently maintained, verified, and validated record of the security decisions made by the cloud service provider over the lifecycle of their cloud service offering.

part.name: overview
part.p: The Independent Assessment Plan rules explain how independent assessment services should approach the verification, validation, and attestation of a cloud service provider's own Security Decision Record on behalf of FedRAMP.

part.name: overview
part.p: The Independent Assessment Report rules explain the minimum expectations for independent assessor attestations after the completion of an independent assessment for FedRAMP Certification.

group.title: Agency Use of FedRAMP Certified Cloud Services (Needs Review)

part.name: overview
part.p: The Agency Use rules summarize the many demands made on agencies by the FedRAMP Authorization Act and OMB Memorandum M-24-15 in a simple, clear, easy-to-follow set of FedRAMP-style rules. These rules align agency policies, authorization letters, machine-readable tools, secure configuration review, continuous monitoring, and communication with FedRAMP so certifications can be reused consistently across government.

part.name: overview
part.p: These rules apply to agencies based on the FedRAMP Authorization Act, OMB M-24-15, and related FedRAMP policies.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST maintain agency-wide internal policies aligned with FedRAMP standards and the principles and directives of OMB Memorandum M-24-15.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST notify FedRAMP after authorizing an in-scope cloud service by supplying the agency Authorization to Operate letter to ato-letter@fedramp.gov.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST ensure that internal governance, risk, compliance, and inventory tools can produce and ingest machine-readable artifacts using formats identified by FedRAMP, including at least:

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies SHOULD encourage adoption of commercial cloud services without incentivizing government-specific services.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST NOT place additional security requirements on FedRAMP Certified cloud service offerings beyond those required by FedRAMP UNLESS the head of the agency or an authorized delegate determines there is a demonstrable need; this does not apply to seeking clarification or asking general questions about FedRAMP Certification Data.

part.name: guidance
part.p: This is related to the Presumption of Adequacy for a FedRAMP Certification.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST notify FedRAMP after requesting any additional information or materials from a FedRAMP Certified cloud service offering beyond those FedRAMP requires by sending an email to info@fedramp.gov.

part.name: guidance
part.p: Agencies are expected to notify FedRAMP under OMB Memorandum M-24-15 section IV (a).

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies SHOULD contribute to FedRAMP lessons-learned reporting, including sharing risk acceptance rationales, to improve government-wide reuse and transparency.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies SHOULD participate in FedRAMP working groups, communities of practice, and stakeholder engagements to supply feedback and align practices across government.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies SHOULD assign at least 1 federal employee to be an active participant in the FedRAMP agency liaison program.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies SHOULD establish and maintain a dedicated shared FedRAMP agency inbox to serve as the official point of contact for communications between FedRAMP and the agency.

part.name: guidance
part.p: A shared FedRAMP agency inbox may follow an agency-specific format such as agency-fedramp@agency.gov.

part.name: overview
part.p: These rules apply when agencies use FedRAMP Certifications to make agency authorization decisions.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST complete an agency authorization before using a cloud service inside a federal information system.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST use the existing FedRAMP Certification package to make agency authorization decisions for FedRAMP Certified cloud services.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MAY accept compensating controls or risk-acceptance decisions in cases of control misalignment between federal and external frameworks.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST collaborate with FedRAMP when discrepancies or conflicts arise between agency-specific security determinations and the baseline FedRAMP Certification package.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST review the Secure Configuration Guides supplied by Providers and configure relevant security settings.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST develop a plan with the Provider to follow Collaborative Continuous Monitoring rules and complete ongoing authorization activities.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST review each Ongoing Authorization Report to understand how changes to the cloud service offering may impact the risk tolerance documented in the agency Authorization to Operate for the federal information system that includes the cloud service offering in its boundary.

part.name: guidance
part.p: This agency review supports agency responsibilities under 44 USC § 35, OMB Circular A-130, FIPS-200, and OMB Memorandum M-24-15.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST notify FedRAMP by sending an email to info@fedramp.gov if information presented in an Ongoing Authorization Report, Quarterly Review, or other ongoing authorization data causes significant concerns that may lead the agency to stop operation of the cloud service offering.

part.name: guidance
part.p: Agencies are expected to notify FedRAMP under OMB Memorandum M-24-15 section IV (a).

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies SHOULD consider the Security Category noted in the agency Authorization to Operate and assign appropriate information security resources for reviewing Ongoing Authorization Reports, attending Quarterly Reviews, and reviewing other ongoing authorization data.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies SHOULD designate a senior information security official to review Ongoing Authorization Reports and represent the agency at Quarterly Reviews for cloud service offerings included in agency information systems.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies SHOULD formally notify the Provider if information presented in an Ongoing Authorization Report, Quarterly Review, or other ongoing authorization data causes significant concerns that may lead the agency to remove the cloud service offering from operation.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies SHOULD review the certification packages and continuous monitoring information for third-party independent information resources, such as other cloud services, that are used by a FedRAMP Certified cloud service when available.

part.name: overview
part.p: These rules apply when an agency sponsors a FedRAMP Rev5 Certification after completing an agency authorization.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST follow the most recent FedRAMP Consolidated Rules when initiating agency-sponsored FedRAMP Certification of in-scope cloud services.

part.name: statement
part.p: Agencies MUST follow the ongoing agency authorization rules after FedRAMP issues a FedRAMP Certification they sponsored.

part.name: statement
part.p: Modifications to the cloud service offering are logged and monitored.

part.name: statement
part.p: Changes to machine-based information resources are executed through the redeployment of version controlled resources rather than direct modification wherever reasonable.

part.name: statement
part.p: The effectiveness of documented change management procedures is persistently reviewed.

part.name: statement
part.p: Persistent testing and validation of changes throughout deployment is automated.

part.name: statement
part.p: The functionality and privileges for infrastructure and services are strictly defined.

part.name: statement
part.p: **Optional:** Automated services are used to persistently assess the security of all machine-based information resources and automatically enforce their intended operational state.

part.name: statement
part.p: Automated services are used to persistently assess the security of all machine-based information resources and automatically enforce their intended operational state.

part.name: statement
part.p: The use and configuration of third-party machine-based information resources is persistently compared against the original provider's best practices and guidance.

part.name: statement
part.p: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they have a minimal attack surface and that lateral movement is minimized if compromised.

part.name: statement
part.p: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they are appropriately optimized for high availability and rapid recovery.

part.name: statement
part.p: Machine-based information resources are persistently reviewed to ensure they are appropriately configured to limit inbound and outbound network traffic.

part.name: statement
part.p: The effectiveness of protection against denial of service attacks and other unwanted activity for machine-based information resources is persistently reviewed.

part.name: statement
part.p: Logical networking and related capabilities are used and persistently reviewed to enforce traffic flow controls.

part.name: statement
part.p: The effectiveness of relevant cybersecurity education and training is persistently reviewed, including at least general training for all employees, role-specific training for employees in high risk roles, training for development and engineering staff on secure software delivery, and training for staff involved with incident response or disaster recovery.

part.name: statement
part.p: The lifecycle and privileges of all accounts, roles, and groups are securely managed using automation.

part.name: statement
part.p: Secure passwordless methods are used for user authentication and authorization when feasible, otherwise strong passwords with phishing-resistant MFA is used.

part.name: statement
part.p: Identity and access management measures are used and persistently reviewed to ensure each user or device can only access the resources they need.

part.name: statement
part.p: A least-privileged, role and attribute-based, and just-in-time security authorization model is used and persistently reviewed for all user and non-user accounts and services.

part.name: statement
part.p: Appropriately secure authentication methods are used and persistently reviewed for non-user accounts and services.

part.name: statement
part.p: Accounts with privileged access are disabled or otherwise secured in response to suspicious activity.

part.name: statement
part.p: Incident after action reports are generated and lessons learned are persistently incorporated.

part.name: statement
part.p: The effectiveness of documented incident response procedures is persistently reviewed.

part.name: statement
part.p: Past incidents are persistently reviewed for patterns or vulnerabilities that were not previously apparent or identified.

part.name: statement
part.p: **Optional:** A least-privileged, role and attribute-based, and just-in-time access authorization model is used and persistently reviewed for access to log data based on organizationally defined data sensitivity.

part.name: statement
part.p: A least-privileged, role and attribute-based, and just-in-time access authorization model is used and persistently reviewed for access to log data based on organizationally defined data sensitivity.

part.name: statement
part.p: The configuration of machine-based information resources, especially infrastructure as code, is persistently evaluated and tested.

part.name: statement
part.p: A list of information resources and event types that will be logged, monitored, and audited is maintained and persistently reviewed to ensure these activities occur.

part.name: statement
part.p: A Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) or similar system(s) is used and persistently reviewed for centralized, tamper-resistant logging of events, activities, and changes.

part.name: statement
part.p: Logs are persistently reviewed and audited.

part.name: statement
part.p: Authoritative sources are used to automatically generate real-time inventories of all information resources when needed.

part.name: statement
part.p: Executive support for achieving the provider's security goals is persistently reviewed and demonstrated.

part.name: statement
part.p: The effectiveness of the provider's investments in achieving security goals is persistently reviewed.

part.name: statement
part.p: The effectiveness of building security and privacy considerations into the Software Development Lifecycle and aligning with CISA Secure By Design principles is persistently reviewed.

part.name: statement
part.p: The effectiveness of the provider's vulnerability disclosure program is persistently reviewed.

part.name: statement
part.p: The alignment of machine-based information resource backups with defined recovery objectives is persistently reviewed.

part.name: statement
part.p: The alignment of recovery plans with defined recovery objectives is persistently reviewed.

part.name: statement
part.p: The desired Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO) are persistently reviewed for alignment with the provider's business needs and capabilities.

part.name: statement
part.p: The capability to recover from incidents and contingencies aligned with defined recovery objectives is persistently tested.

part.name: statement
part.p: The configuration of machine-based information resources is managed using automation and persistently reviewed for drift.

part.name: statement
part.p: Management, protection, and regular rotation of digital keys, certificates, and other secrets is automated and persistently reviewed.

part.name: statement
part.p: Information resources are persistently evaluated for opportunities to improve security and those improvements are persistently made.

part.name: statement
part.p: **Optional:** Plans, procedures, and the state of information resources are persistently reviewed after making changes to limit and remove unwanted residual elements that would likely negatively affect the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of federal customer data.

part.name: statement
part.p: Plans, procedures, and the state of information resources are persistently reviewed after making changes to limit and remove unwanted residual elements that would likely negatively affect the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of federal customer data.

part.name: statement
part.p: **Optional:** Unwanted federal customer data is removed promptly when requested by an agency in alignment with customer agreements, including from backups if appropriate; this typically applies when a customer spills information or when a customer seeks to remove information from a service due to a change in usage.

part.name: statement
part.p: Unwanted federal customer data is removed promptly when requested by an agency in alignment with customer agreements, including from backups if appropriate; this typically applies when a customer spills information or when a customer seeks to remove information from a service due to a change in usage.

part.name: statement
part.p: Encrypt or otherwise secure network traffic.

part.name: statement
part.p: **Optional:** The authenticity and integrity of communications between machine-based information resources is persistently validated using automation.

part.name: statement
part.p: The authenticity and integrity of communications between machine-based information resources is persistently validated using automation.

part.name: statement
part.p: Use cryptographic methods to validate the integrity of machine-based information resources.

part.name: statement
part.p: Persistently identify, review, and mitigate potential supply chain risks.

part.name: statement
part.p: Third party software information resources are automatically monitored for upstream vulnerabilities using mechanisms that may include contractual notification requirements or active monitoring services.

resource.title: FedRAMP Consolidated Rules for 2026 Public Preview

citation.text: NIST FIPS 199 Standards for Security Categorization of Federal Information and Information Systems

resource.title: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency Federal Incident Notification Guidelines
FedRAMP Marketplace Provider Listing Request Form
